treatments.treatmentTitle: Choanolaimus sparsiporus Leduc & Zhao, 2025, sp. nov.
treatments.articleTitle: Three new nematode species (Chromadorida: Selachinematidae) from the continental slope of New Zealand

images.captionText: Fig. 5. Choanolaimus sparsiporus sp. nov., holotype, ♂ (NIWA 181632) A. Anterior body region. B. Posterior body region. C. Entire specimen. Arrows show position of dorsosublateral pore complexes. Scale bar: A = 75 µm; B = 60 µm; C = 200 µm.

response.answer:
According to Document 1:

Choanolaimus sparsiporus can be distinguished from other species by the following characteristics:
- Large amphidial fovea, 0.56 cbd wide with 5¼ turns
- Arrangement of pore complexes (dorsosublateral vs dorso- and ventrosublateral in C. psammophilus)
- Shape of the inner labial sensilla (papilliform vs ‘flap-like’ or on swollen lobes in C. psammophilus)
- Pharynx with posterior bulb
- Longer spicules (1.5 vs <1.0 body diameters at level of cloacal opening in other species)
- Number of precloacal supplements (two vs 3–4 in C. serieporus)
